questions.q:
Let

 A=x,y∈R×R∣2x2+2y2-2x-2y=1

B=x,y∈R×R∣4x2+4y2-16y+7=0 and

C=x,y∈R×R∣x2+y2-4x-2y+5≤r2. Then the minimum value of r such that A∪B⊆C is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

S1:x2+y2-x-y-12=0

So, the centre is C1:12, 12 and the radius is r1=14+14+12=1

S2:x2+y2-4y+74=0

So, the centre is C2:0,2 and the radius is 

r2=4-74=32

S3:x2+y2-4x-2y+5-r2=0

So, the centre is C3:2,1 and the radius is 

r3=4+1-5+r2=r

From the diagram, we can say that if A∪B⊆C, then the circle A & B should lie in the circle C

Here, C1C3=52

So, 52≤r-1⇒r≤1-52r≥1+52

Also, C2C3=5≤r-32

⇒r≥5+32r≤32-5

So, the minimum value of r=3+252
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: The locus of the middle points of the chords of the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> which subtend an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math> at the centre of the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math>, is a circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></msup></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

C1: x-42+y-52=4 which subtend an angle θi at the centre of the circle Ci, is a circle of radius ri,

Now plotting the diagram we get,

Now given If θ1=π3, θ3=2π3 and r12=r22+r32,

Now taking triangle CPB we get,

cosθ2=PC2, so PC=2cosθ2

Now using distance formula we get,

h-42+k-52=4cos2θ2

So, locus will be,

x-42+y-52=4cos2θ2

Now radius r1=2cosπ6=3, r2=2cosθ22 and r3=2cosπ3=1

Now using r12=r22+r32 we get,

32=r22+12

⇒r22=2

⇒4cos2θ2=2

⇒cosθ2=12

⇒θ=π2

questions.q: The points of intersection of the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>≠</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. The image of the circle with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> as a diameter in the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is :
questions.solution:
Given,

The points of intersection of the line ax+by=0,(a≠b) and the circle x2+y2-2x=0 are Aα,0 and B(1,β) then,

For point Aα,0

a(α)+b(0)=0⇒α=0

And, α2-2α=0⇒α=0,2

For point B(1,β)

a+bβ=0

Or, β=-ba

And, 1+β2-2=0

⇒β=±1

∵a≠b therefore,

Only possibility α=0, β=1

Points are A0,0 and B1,1

So the centre of the circle with diameter AB is 12,12 and radius r=12

Now the image of 12,12 about the line x+y+2=0 is,

x-121=y-121=-212+12+212+12

⇒x-121=y-121=-3

⇒Centre of image circle is -52,-52

Equation of image circle

x+522+y+522=122

⇒x2+y2+5x+5y+12=0

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> be a circle passing through the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. The line segment <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> is not a diameter of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> is the radius of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> and its centre lies on the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>13</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Equation of line joining AB is 

y+1=5x-2

i.e. 5x-y-11=0 

Also AB=3-22+4+12=26

r is the radius of the circle C

For the circle x-52+y-12=132

centre is 5,1 and radius R=132

Equation of perpendicular bisector of AB will be y-1=-15x-5⇒x+5y-10=0

Solving this equation of line with x-52+y-12=132, we get 

M as 52,32 

So M lies on the line joining AB.

Now r2=CM2+AM2

=2×1322+1322

r2=652

questions.q: A point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> moves so that the sum of squares of its distances from the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> be the locus of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math>, which intersects the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis at the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi></math> and the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math>-axis at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>D</mi></math>. Then the area of the quadrilateral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>D</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

A point P moves so that the sum of squares of its distances from the points 1,2 and -2,1 is 14. 

So, x-12+y-22+x+22+y-12=14

⇒x2+y2+x-3y-2=0

Put x=0

⇒y2-3y-2=0

⇒y=3±172

Put y=0

⇒x2+x-2=0

x+2x-1=0

∴  A-2,0,B1,0,C0,3+172,D0,3-172

Area formed by ACBD will be =12-2003+1721003-172-20

=12-3-17-32-172+32-172+3-17

=12×3×17=3172 sq units.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: The set of all values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for which the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> bisects two distinct chords drawn from a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> on the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>)</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>)</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, is equal to :
questions.solution:
Given,

The line y=-x bisect the chord PQ

If λ,-λ is the mid-point of the chord PQ then,

Q(2λ-α,-2λ-β)

Given circle 2x2+2y2-(1+a)x-(1-a)y=0

Or, x2+y2-1+a2x-1-a2y=0

Let 1+a2=α,1-a2=β

The circle equation will be,

x2+y2-αx-βy=0

Point Q will lie on the circle,

(2λ-α)2+(-2λ-β)2-α(2λ-α)-β(-2λ-β)=0

⇒4λ2+3λ(β-α)+α2+β2=0

So, quadratic in λ should have D>0

⇒9(β-α)2-4(4)α2+β2>0

Put the value of α and β we get,

⇒9a2-161+a22>0

⇒a2>8
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Consider ellipses <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>k</mi></msub><mo>:</mo><mi>k</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>k</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mn>20</mn></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math> be the circle which touches the four chords joining the end points (one on minor axis and another on major axis) of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math> is the radius of the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msubsup><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
Given,

Ek: kx2+k2y2=1

⇒Ek :x21k2+y21k2=1

Now equation of the chord joining the points 1k,0 & 0,1k will be,

Lk:x1k+y1k=1

⇒kx+ky-1=0

Now rk= Perpendicular distance of Lk from (0,0) we get,

rk=-1k+k2

⇒rk2=1k+k2

Now putting the value of rk2 in ∑k=1201rk2 we get,

∑k=1201rk2=∑k=120k+k2=20×212+20×21×416

=210+2870=3080
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: If the co-ordinates of two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><msqrt><mn>7</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>7</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> respectively and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is any point on the conic, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>16</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>144</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>P</mi><mi>B</mi></math> is equal to :
questions.solution:
For ellipse x216+y29=1

Comparing with, standard equation of ellipse x2a2+y2b2=1, we get  

a=4,b=3.

∴e=1-916=74
Since, A and B are foci

Therefore, PA+PB=2a

=2×4=8.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: If the normal at an end of latus rectum of an ellipse passes through an extremity of the minor axis, then the eccentricity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math> of the ellipse satisfies :
questions.solution:
Equation of normal at ae,b2a

a2xae-b2yb2/a=a2-b2

it passes through (0,-b)

ab=a2e2

a2b2=a4e4        ∵b2=a2(1-e2

1-e2=e4

questions.q: The locus of the mid-point of the line segment joining the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and the points on the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> is an ellipse with eccentricity
questions.solution:
Given,

x24+y22=1

Let coordinate of D 2cosθ+42,2sinθ+32≡h,k

So, 2h-42=cosθ      ...1

And 2k-32=sinθ      ...2

Equation 12+equation 22, then we get

2h-422+2k-322=1⇒x-221+y-32212=1

∴  Required eccentricity is e=1-12=12

questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> be a focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi></math> such that it subtends an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Let the line segment <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> be also a focal chord of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi><mo>:</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>></mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>e</mi></math> is the eccentricity of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Since PQ is focal chord of the parabola y2=4ax

so let Pt2,2t, Q1t2,-2t and R3,0

Given mPR·mPQ=-1

2tt2-3×-2t1t2-3=-1

t2-12=0

⇒t=1

i.e. PQ must be the latus rectum

We get P1,2 & Q1,-2

For ellipse

2b2a=4 & ae=1

Also b2=a21-e2

∴ a=1+2

and e2=3-22

Hence 1e2=13-22=3+22
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A ray of light through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is reflected at a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> on the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo></math> axis and then passes through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> If this reflected ray is the directrix of an ellipse with eccentricity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> and the distance of the nearer focus from this directrix is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>8</mn><msqrt><mn>53</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then the equation of the other directrix can be:
questions.solution:
The reflected point of 2,1 is -2,1.

Equation of reflected ray

y-1=3-15+2x+2

⇒y-1=27x+2

⇒7y-7=2x+4

⇒2x-7y+11=0

Let the equation of other directrix is

2x-7y+λ=0

Distance of directrix from focus

ae-ae=853, where e is the eccentricity

⇒3a-a3=853 or a=353

Distance from other focus ae+ae

=3a+a3=10a3=103×353=1053

Distance between two directrix =2ae

=2×3×353=1853

So, λ-1153=1853

⇒λ-11=18 or -18

⇒2x-7y-7=0 or 2x-7y+29=0
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>e</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> respectively be the eccentricity and length of the latus rectum of the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>e</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>l</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math> respectively the eccentricity and length of the latus rectum of its conjugate hyperbola. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>11</mn><mn>14</mn></mfrac><mi>l</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>11</mn><mn>8</mn></mfrac><msup><mi>l</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>77</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>44</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
By using eccentricty formula we get,

e=1+b2a2,l=2b2a

Given e2=1114l

So, 1+b2a2=1114·2b2a

a2+b2a2=117·b2a     ⋯1

Also e'=1+a2 b2,l'=2a2 b

Given e'2=118l'

1+a2b2=118·2a2b

a2+b2b2=114·a2b     ⋯2

Now equation 1÷equation 2 we get,

b2a2=47·b3a3

∴7a=4b     ⋯3

From 2

16b249+b2b2=114·16b249b

∴ b=4×6511×16      ⋯4

We have to find value of

77a+44b

So, 117a+4b=114b+4b=11×8b

∴ Value of 11×8b=11×8×4×6516×11=130

questions.q: For <math><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> let the curves <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>y</mi></math> intersect at origin <math><mi>O</mi></math> and a point <math><mi>P</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Let the line <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>b</mi><mo><</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> intersect the chord <math><mi>O</mi><mi>P</mi></math> and the <math><mi>x</mi></math> -axis at points <math><mi>Q</mi></math> and <math><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> respectively. If the line <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi></math> bisects the area bounded by the curves, <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo></math> and the area of <math><mo>∆</mo><mi>O</mi><mi>Q</mi><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then ‘ <math><mi>a</mi></math> ’ satisfies the equation:
questions.solution:
∫0bax-x2adx=a26

⇒ 23ab32-b33a=a26           ...1

Also area of ΔOQR=12 

12b2=12⇒b=1

Put in 1

⇒ 4aa-2=a3

⇒ a6+4a3+4=16a3

⇒ a6-12a3+4=0

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> be a variable point on the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> Then, the locus of the mid-point of the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and the foot of the perpendicular drawn from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> to the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi></math> is:
questions.solution:
We have,

y=4x2+1

L:y=x

Let the foot of perpendicular from P to line y=x is Q.

Let P≡x,y, Q≡c,c and R≡h,k where, R is the mid-point of PQ. 

Clearly,

PQ⊥L

⇒k-ch-c=-1

⇒c=h+k2

And, 

R≡x+c2,y+c2

⇒R≡x2+h4+k4,y2+h4+k4

Hence,

h=x2+h4+k4⇒x=3h2-k2

k=y2+h4+k4⇒y=3k2-h2

Now,

y=4x2+1

⇒3k-h2=43h-k22+1

⇒3k-h=23h-k2+2

Required locus is

23x-y2+x-3y+2=0

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>t</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> be a conic. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> be the focus and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> be the point on the axis of the conic such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mi>A</mi><mo>⊥</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>A</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is any point on the conic. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> is the ordinate of the centroid of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Δ</mi><mi>S</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>t</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></munder><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
The parametric form x=2t,y=t23 represents the parabola x2=12y

 

Given AS⊥AB

So, mAS·mAB=-1

⇒3-t230-2t·α-t230-2t=-1

 ⇒3α=27t2+t4t2-9

Ordinate of centroid of ΔSAB=k=α+t23+33=9+3α+t29

limt→1k=limt→1199+t2+27t2+t4t2-9=1318

questions.q: If the length of the latus rectum of a parabola, whose focus is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and the tangent at its vertex is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi></math>, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>a</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given the equation of tangent at vertex is x+y=a

and the focus is a,a

Now the perpendicular distance from focus to tangent is 

a+a-a2=a2

Also given length of latus rectum=16

i.e. a2=164=4

⇒a=42

questions.q: A triangle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> lying in the first quadrant has two vertices as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. If<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∠</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>90</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mo>,</mo></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>ar</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>Δ</mi><mi>ABC</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> sq. units, then the abscissa of the vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is :
questions.solution:
mAC=β−2α−1

mAB=2−11−3=−12

AB⊥AC

∴ β−2α−1−12=−1

β=2α−2+2

β=2α

Now area of ΔABC=55=12AB.AC

⇒ 123−12+1−22.α−12+β−22=55

⇒ α−12+2α−22=10

⇒ α−125=10

⇒ α−1=25

⇒ α=1±25.

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> be given three points. A line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>m</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> , intersects lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> at point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> respectively. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>A</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be the areas of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Δ</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Δ</mi><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi><mi>C</mi></math> respectively, such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>A</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><msub><mi>A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is equal to :
questions.solution:
P≡x1,mx1

 Q≡x2,mx2

A1=12341201-111=132

A2=12x1mx11x2mx21201

A2=122mx1-mx2=mx1-x2

A1=3 A2⇒132=3 mx1-x2

⇒  x1-x2=166 m

AC: x+3 y=2

BC: y=4 x-8

P:x+3y=2 & y=mx⇒x1=21+3m

Q: y=4x-8 & y=mx⇒x2=84-m

x1-x2=21+3m-84-m

=-26 m1+3m4-m=26 m3m+1m-4

=26 m3m+14-m

x1-x2=136 m

26 m3m+14-m=136 m

⇒  12m2=-3m+1m-4

⇒  12m2=-3m2-11m-4

⇒  15m2-11m-4=0

⇒  15m2-15m+4m-4=0

⇒  15m+4m-1=0

⇒  m=1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> be a triangle with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∠</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>. If the equation of the median through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and the equation of angle bisector of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
Let, x-coordinate of the point C is α, So coordinate of the point   Cα,7α-14  ∵(C lies on 7x-4y-1=0)

 Hence, mid point of AC is mα-32,7α+38

As, point m lies on the line 2x+y-3=0, Substituting, we get α=3.

So. C(3,5)

Hence, slope of AC is 23 and slope of 7x-4y-1=0 is74

∴tanθ2=74-231+74×23=12

⇒tanθ=2·121-14=43⇒tanθ=43

questions.q: The equations of the sides <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mi>A</mi></math> of a triangle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>39</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> respectively and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is its circumcentre. Then which of the following is NOT true
questions.solution:
Intersection of 2x+y=0 & x-y=3 is A1,-2

Perpendicular bisector of AB is y-3=12x-2⇒x-2y+4=0

Take image of A along the perpendicular bisector of AB, we get 

x-11=y+2-2=-295=-185

i.e. x=-135, y=265

 B-135,265

This point lies on the line x+py=39

i.e. -135+p265=39⇒-1+2p=15

⇒p=8

Solving x+8y=39 and x-y=3, we get C7,4

Now AC2=1-72+-2-42=72=9p

and AC2+p2=72+64=136

Also ΔABC=121-21741-1352651

=1218+18×135

=9185=1625=32.4∉34,38

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be the slopes of two adjacent sides of a square of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>11</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi>m</mi><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>220</mn></math>. If one vertex of the square is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> and the equation of one diagonal is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>cosα</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>sinα</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>72</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given m1 & m2 are slopes of two adjacent sides of square then, m1m2=-1

Also given a2+11a+3m12+m22=220

 ⇒a2+11a+3m12+1m12=220

Now on plotting the diagram we get,

Now given equation of AC,

cosα-sinαx+sinα+cosαy=10

Now by property of square we know that diagonals are perpendicular to each other 

So equation of BD will be sinα+cosαx+sinα-cosαy+λ=0 now is passes through D10cosα-sinα,10sinα-cosα,

So, equation of  BD=sinα+cosαx+sinα-cosαy=0

Now slope of AB & AD will be given by angle bisector equation of AC & BD

cosα-sinαx+sinα+cosαy-102=±sinα+cosαx+sinα-cosαy2

Taking positive sign we get,

-2sinαx+2cosαy-10=0, so slope m1=tanα

Now taking negative sign we get,

2cosαx+2sinαy-10=0, so slope m2=-cotα

Now finding a by using distance of point from line 

Now we can see distance of D from AC will be a2,

So, 10cosα-sinα2+10cosα-sinα2-102=a2

⇒a=10

Now putting the value of a, m1 & m2 in a2+11a+3m12+1m12=220 we get,

⇒100+110+3tan2α+cot2α=220

⇒210+3tan2α+cot2α=220

⇒3tan2α+cot2α=10

⇒tan2α+cot2α=103

So this is only possible when tan2α=3 & cot2α=13 or vice versa

So, tan2α=3⇒α=π3

So, 72sin4α+cos4α+a2-3a+13

=72916+116+100-30+13

=7258+83=45+83=128

questions.q: If the orthocentre of the triangle, whose vertices are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, then the quadratic equation whose roots are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math>, is
questions.solution:
We have,

Here mBH×mAC=-1

⇒β-3α-21-2=-1

⇒β-3=2α-4

⇒β=2α-1        ....(1)

mAH×mBC=-1

⇒β-2α-1-2=-1

⇒2β-4=α-1

Using (1), we can write:

⇒2(2α-1)-4=α-1

⇒22α-1=α+3

⇒3α=5

α=53, β=73⇒H53,73

α+4β=53+283=333=11

β+4α=73+203=273=9

Required equation is

x2-20x+99=0

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> be the mid-points of the sides of a triangle with incentre at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math>. If the focus of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> passing through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are rational numbers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

A(0,1), B(1,1) and C(1,0) be the mid-points of the sides of a triangle with incentre at the point D,

Now finding the vertices of the triangle by using midpoint formula and plotting the diagram we get,

Now finding the incentre of the above triangle using the formula,

I≡ax1+bx2+cx3a+b+c,ay1+by2+cy3a+b+c

We get, Incentre D=44+22,44+22

Now given parabola y2=4ax passes through the incentre D we get,

44+222=4a44+22

⇒a=14+22

Now we know that focus of parabola is given by, focus (a, 0)

Hence, focus will be,14+22,0=4-228,0


Now comparing with (α+β2,0) we get,

α=48=12, β=-14
Hence, αβ2=12-142=162=8
